pending.body: Fix aborted-task cleanup so a non-cancellation error from finish_cancelled_dispatch() does not strand the task lease. finish_cancelled_dispatch() now returns backend/interrupted-state cleanup errors (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3161-3255), but both aborted-task branches call allow_aborted_dispatch_fast_path() before label sync / release_task_lease() (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:77-82, 912-929, 1620-1623). For a task-local abort, any Io/corrupt cleanup error exits early with the task already marked Aborted, its lease still attached, and in multi-repo mode label_dirty still false, so Phase 0 never revisits it and the project stays blocked until TTL-based reconcile.

Run stale backend-process cleanup when repairing an interrupted daemon handoff after the daemon dies mid-shutdown. repair_missing_interrupted_handoff_run_failed_event() only restores the missing run_failed event (src/cli/run.rs:2051-2101), and both load_dispatch_run_snapshot() and handle_resume() consume that repaired snapshot without calling cleanup_stale_backend_process_groups() (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2918-2931; src/cli/run.rs:3304-3360). If the daemon is SIGKILLed after persisting the handoff but before finish_cancelled_dispatch() reaches backend cleanup, the orphaned backend process group survives and a resumed/retried run can start a second backend alongside it.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 13 -> Plan and Implement
